_meta.description: Single source of truth for leaf descriptions used in 03 prompts (condition R0b).

fields.general: Plain-text definition of the leaf, used in prompt's description block.
fields.indicators: List of distinctive features used by the LLM to disambiguate.

Phishing/Classified-Ad-Phishing.general: Phishing scams targeting users of classified ad platforms (Anibis, Tutti, Ricardo). The scammer poses as a buyer or seller and uses fake payment or delivery confirmation to steal credentials or money.
indicators: References to selling/buying on Tutti, Anibis, Ricardo
Move conversation to WhatsApp or SMS

Phishing/Parcel-Notification-Phishing.general: Fake parcel delivery notifications impersonating DPD, Swiss Post, DHL, UPS. Victim is asked to pay a small fee for redelivery or confirm address.
indicators: Carrier names: DPD, Swiss Post, DHL, UPS, FedEx

Phishing/Refund-Phishing.general: Fake refund notifications from health insurance, transport services, tax authorities, or banks (CSS, Helsana, SwissPass, SBB, Steuerverwaltung). Requests bank details to receive a refund.
indicators: Health insurance, SBB, tax authority, bank impersonation
Bank credentials or card details requested

Phishing/Other-Phishing.general: Generic phishing not fitting other categories: bank account verification, government service impersonation, social media/email account compromise warnings, SwissID, etc.

Phishing/Vishing/Callback-Scam.general: Phishing where the victim receives a FAKE INVOICE OR CHARGE notification (email or SMS) and is prompted to CALL BACK a phone number where scammers operate. Victim initiates the call.
indicators: Email/SMS announcing a charge already made
Phone number to call for cancellation/dispute
Apple, Norton, McAfee, PayPal impersonation often used

Phishing/Vishing/Other-Vishing.general: Voice phishing where THE SCAMMER CALLS the victim directly, impersonating bank, police, government agency, tech support. Victim does NOT initiate the call.
indicators: Cold phone calls from spoofed numbers
Impersonation of police, customs, bank, EWZ, court
Pressure to transfer money or share credentials
Scammer initiates the call to victim

Malware/Ransomware.general: Malicious software encrypting victim's files or systems and demanding cryptocurrency ransom for decryption keys or to prevent data leak.
indicators: Ransomware family names: LockBit, BlackCat, Conti, Akira

Fraud/Fake-Sextortion/With-Password.general: Extortion emails claiming hacked webcam/device, EXPLICITLY MENTIONING a real or leaked password of the victim as proof of compromise. Demands Bitcoin payment.
indicators: Email contains the victim's actual or leaked password
Claims of webcam recording, browsing history capture
Threats to send compromising material to contacts

Fraud/Fake-Sextortion/Other-Fake-Sextort.general: Generic extortion emails claiming compromising material/webcam access WITHOUT mentioning any specific password. Demands payment in cryptocurrency.
indicators: Claims of webcam capture, browsing tracking
NO password mentioned (key distinction from With-Password)
Threats to send material to family/contacts
May claim email sent 'from your own account' (header spoofing)

Fraud/Fake-Support/Popup.general: Tech support scam INITIATED BY A POPUP or fake browser alert claiming infection. Popup prompts victim to call a fake support number.
indicators: Web popup or full-screen browser alert
Victim is browsing when popup appears

Fraud/Fake-Support/Other-Fake-Support.general: Tech support scam where SCAMMER CONTACTS victim via PHONE or EMAIL (not popup). Pretends to be Microsoft, Apple, ISP technical support.

Fraud/Investment-Fraud/Pig-Butchering.general: Long-con scam combining ROMANCE + CRYPTOCURRENCY investment. Scammer builds trust through dating apps/social media over weeks/months, then introduces fake crypto trading platform.
indicators: Match on Tinder/dating app or WhatsApp introduction
Fake crypto platforms with initial fake profits shown

Fraud/Investment-Fraud/Other-Investment-.general: Investment scams WITHOUT romance component: fake trading platforms, Ponzi schemes, fake brokers, fake ICOs. Cold contact via phone, email, or social media ads.
indicators: Promises of high daily returns (e.g., 3% per day)
